twitter_thread: 87% of AI partnerships fail to deliver on promises, but Ceva's latest deal with a major US software company bucks the trend.
Ceva's AI cores are fine-tuned for tasks like image recognition (97% accuracy) and language processing (92% accuracy) using TensorFlow and PyTorch.
Retraining AI models for new use cases can cost up to $150k, a mistake Ceva avoided by prioritizing domain-specific AI.
By optimizing for specific domains, Ceva achieved a 25% boost in performance, outpacing industry averages.
Their secret sauce? A combination of quantization (30% model compression) and knowledge distillation (20% accuracy gain).
As AI integrations become more complex, what's the single most important factor: high-quality training data or sophisticated model architecture?
Have you had to retrain an AI model from scratch or drastically adjust your data pipeline? What did you learn from the experience? #aiintegration #aicore
